info.title: remote-watch: Listening for target assembly name...
info.description:
Environment variables:
DOTNET_USE_POLLING_FILE_WATCHER
When set to '1' or 'true', dotnet-watch will poll the file system for
changes. This is required for some file systems, such as network shares,
Docker mounted volumes, and other virtual file systems.
DOTNET_WATCH
dotnet-watch sets this variable to '1' on all child processes launched.
DOTNET_WATCH_ITERATION
dotnet-watch sets this variable to '1' and increments by one each time
a file is changed and the command is restarted.
DOTNET_WATCH_SUPPRESS_EMOJIS
When set to '1' or 'true', dotnet-watch will not show emojis in the
console output.
Remarks:
The special option '--' is used to delimit the end of the options and
the beginning of arguments that will be passed to the child dotnet process.
For example: dotnet watch -- --verbose run
Even though '--verbose' is an option dotnet-watch supports, the use of '--'
indicates that '--verbose' should be treated instead as an argument for
dotnet-run.

options.name: --quiet
options.description: Suppresses all output except warnings and errors

options.name: --list
options.description: Lists all discovered files without starting the watcher.

options.name: --no-hot-reload
options.description: Suppress hot reload for supported apps.

options.name: --non-interactive
options.description:
Runs dotnet-watch in non-interactive mode. This option is only supported when running with Hot Reload enabled.
Use this option to prevent console input from being captured.

options.name: --configuration
options.description: The configuration to run for. The default for most projects is 'Debug'.

options.name: --framework
options.description: The target framework to run for. The target framework must also be specified in the project file.

options.name: --interactive
options.description: Allows the command to stop and wait for user input or action (for example to complete authentication).

options.name: --no-restore
options.description: Do not restore the project before building.

options.name: --self-contained
options.description:
Publish the .NET runtime with your application so the runtime doesn't need to be installed on the target machine.
The default is 'false.' However, when targeting .NET 7 or lower, the default is 'true' if a runtime identifier is specified.

options.name: --no-self-contained
options.description: Publish your application as a framework dependent application. A compatible .NET runtime must be installed on the target machine to run your application.

options.name: -verbosity
options.description: Set the MSBuild verbosity level. Allowed values are q[uiet], m[inimal], n[ormal], d[etailed], and diag[nostic].

options.name: --runtime
options.description: The target runtime to run for.

options.name: --disable-build-servers
options.description: Force the command to ignore any persistent build servers.

options.name: --artifacts-path
options.description: The artifacts path. All output from the project, including build, publish, and pack output, will go in subfolders under the specified path.
